headline: FedEx announces a domestic operations transformation program in France and appoints Scott L. Ray as COO of US and Canada ahead of the FedEx Freight spin-off.
investor_impact: The restructuring in France highlights FedEx's continued focus on business optimization and margin expansion, despite near-term cash headwinds of $175M-$275M. Furthermore, the executive appointments provide clear leadership succession for the core business as it prepares for the strategic spin-off of FedEx Freight.
key_figures: Up to 500 operational positions reduced in France
Up to 800 operational team members facing location and schedule changes
$175 million to $275 million in expected pre-tax restructuring costs
18-month expected execution timeline for the transformation program
Charges to be incurred through fiscal 2028
Scott L. Ray appointed COO, US and Canada - Elect effective February 1, 2026, and COO effective June 1, 2026
John A. Smith to become President and CEO of FedEx Freight following its spin-off expected June 1, 2026
